The '''''Singularity''''' was an [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyer]] under the command of [[Director of Imperial Intelligence|Imperial Intelligence Director]] and [[Emperor's Hand/Legends|Emperor's Hand]] [[Cronal]], better known as [[Blackhole]], during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

==Characteristics==
The ''Singularity'' was an [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyer]] manufactured by [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]]. Coated with a rare [[Stygian-triprismatic polymer/Legends|stygian-triprismatic polymer]], the ''Singularity'' was almost impossible to detect at [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] range and nearly invisible to the naked eye in deep [[Realspace/Legends|space]]. The vessel also possessed a gravity well generator allowing it to interdict hyperspace travel.<ref name="TEP" />

==History==
The ship served as [[Director of Imperial Intelligence|Imperial Intelligence Director]] [[Cronal|Blackhole]]'s main base of operations after he fled [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] while being possessed with an irrational fear of being in danger. The ''Singularity'', its attached [[TIE series/Legends|TIE]] [[Fighter wing/Legends|fighter wing]] the [[123rd Nightstalker Group]], and Blackhole's own [[Shadow stormtrooper/Legends|special stormtrooper division]] were all cast in obsidian to match his eccentricities.<ref name="TEP" />

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Singularity'' was first mentioned in ''[[The Emperor's Pawns]]'', an article written by [[Abel G. Peña]] and [[Pablo Hidalgo]] and published in [[Star Wars Gamer 5|''Star Wars Gamer'' 5]]<ref name="TEP" /> on [[July 24]], [[2001]].<ref name="SW.com">{{SWArchive|url=gaming/other/rpg/news20010627.html|text=''Gamer'' #5. A Domain of Evil it is.}}</ref> The Singularity was only mentioned in the section of ''The Emperor's Pawns'' that covered Blackhole,<ref name="TEP" /> which was written by Hidalgo.<ref name="blog2">{{Blog|abelgpena|69|The Emperor's Pawns: A Retrospective}}</ref>
